get.description:

For non-UPM clients, this resource returns all categories regardless of the specified application.

get.description: Get a list of apps matching the specified parameters.

parameters.name: application
parameters.description: Only returns apps compatible with this application

parameters.name: applicationBuild
parameters.description: Only returns apps compatible with the specified application build number

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Only returns apps with any of the specified categories

parameters.name: cost
parameters.description: Only returns apps with the specified payment model

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Return apps filtered or sorted using the specified parameter

parameters.name: forThisUser
parameters.description: Only returns apps from vendors associated with the current user

parameters.name: hosting
parameters.description: Only returns apps with the specified hosting model

parameters.name: cloudComplianceBoundaries
parameters.description: Only returns apps with the specified cloud compliance boundaries, with "commercial" as the default value for cloud apps if not provided explicitly. This parameter must be left null for non-cloud apps.

parameters.name: includeHidden
parameters.description: Includes apps that are normally hidden in the site

parameters.name: includePrivate
parameters.description: Includes private apps or versions if you are authorized to see them

parameters.name: marketingLabel
parameters.description: Only returns apps with the specified marketing labels

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Only returns apps that match the search text

parameters.name: withVersion
parameters.description: Includes the latest compatible version in the response

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: If specified, skips ahead by this number of items

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: If specified, limits the result set to this number of items

post.description:
Create a new app.

The associated vendor account must exist prior to the app's creation. 

Note that the app's first version should be included in the request body (in `_embedded.version`).

This resource requires authentication.

This resource requires authentication.

put.description:
Approve, reject or resubmit an app for approval.

Vendors can use this resource to resubmit a previously rejected app for approval. This is done by setting the `status` field to `pending`.

Administrators can use this resource to approve or reject an app that is submitted for approval.

This resource requires authentication.

get.description:
Get pricing for the specified app. 

Live pricing is visible to all users. Pending pricing is only visible if you are authenticated as a vendor contact.

parameters.name: addonKey
parameters.description: The unique identifier for this app, for example "com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-questions"

parameters.name: liveOrPending
parameters.description: Specifies either the pricing that is currently in effect and visible to users (live), or pending that will take effect after a delay

parameters.name: cloudComplianceBoundary
parameters.description: The cloud compliance boundary for which the pricing is to be fetched, with "commercial" as the default for cloud apps. Must be left null for non-cloud apps.

404.description: The app is unknown or it does not have pricing of the specified type

put.description:
Update pricing for the specified app.

Updated pricing is always initially in the "pending" state.

For an app pending approval, pricing changes will go "live" when the app is approved. For an already-approved app, pricing changes will go "live" after 24 hours.

This resource requires authentication.

parameters.name: addonKey
parameters.description: The unique identifier for this app, for example "com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-questions"

parameters.name: liveOrPending
parameters.description: Specifies either the pricing that is currently in effect and visible to users (live), or pending that will take effect after a delay

get.summary: Get app privacy and security information
get.description: Get the privacy and security info for the specified app. This resource requires authentication for fetching "draft" version of privacy and security information.

parameters.name: addonKey
parameters.description: The unique identifier for this app, for example "com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-questions".

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Specifies the version of Privacy and security information to be fetched. Possible values are "live" or "draft". For partners with manage app permissions, if a draft version exists then the default value will be "draft", or else the default value will be "live".

404.description: The App/live form does not exist.

put.description:
Release an app after it has been approved.

If a vendor requests that their app is *not* automatically released after it has been approved, they can use this resource to release their app.

Set the `released` field to `true`. This only works one-way; vendors cannot set it to `false` later.

This resource requires authentication.

get.description: Get links to permitted asset resources.

post.description:
Use this resource to upload an app artifact (`.jar` or `.obr`) to Marketplace, so it can be used by an app version.

The request body is the file data.

If successful, the resource will respond with a resource link in `_links.self` that can be used as the `artifact` link when creating an app or app version. The response may include additional properties; other than `fileInfo`, these are used internally and should be ignored.

This resource requires authentication.

Example: This curl command uploads the artifact present in a file ('my-file.jar').
``` bash
curl --request POST \
  --url https://marketplace.atlassian.com/rest/2/assets/artifact \
  --user 'email@example.com:<api_token>' \
  --header 'Accept: application/json' \
  --header 'Content-Type: multipart/form-data' \
  --form file=@"my-file.jar"
```

file.description: The relative location and name of the artifact file to be uploaded

post.description:
Use this resource to copy and validate an app artifact (`.jar` or `.obr`) that is available from an external URL, so it can be used by an app version.

If successful, you will get a resource link in `_links.self` that can be used as the `artifact` link when creating an app or app version.

Note that this step can be skipped by just setting the `artifact` link to the external URL where the artifact is hosted when creating an app version; Marketplace will then download the artifact automatically.

This resource requires authentication.

parameters.name: uri
parameters.description: Marketplace will attempt to download the artifact from this location

post.description:
Use this resource to upload an image (`.jpg`, `.png`, `.gif`, `.bmp`) to Marketplace, so you can use it for an app or vendor.

The request body is the file data.

If successful, you will get a resource link in _links.self that you can use as the appropriate image link in another resource, for example the `logo` link if you are creating an app.

Note that if the image is available from an external URL, you can skip this step by just setting the image link tothat external URL when you are creating the resource that uses it.

parameters.name: imageType
parameters.description: Specifies what this image will be used for, which determines the allowable image size

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: The logical filename

get.description:
Returns details about a previously uploaded file that Marketplace is now storing.

The asset can either be an image file or an app artifact. If it is an app artifact, the response may include additional properties which are used internally and should be ignored.

parameters.name: assetName
parameters.description: The unique identifying string that Marketplace uses to refer to this asset

delete.description:
Removes a file from Marketplace storage which had been previously uploaded.

This is only allowed if:

1. you, rather than another user, uploaded the file, and
2. the asset is not currently being used by any other resource (for example, it cannot be the logo image for an existing app).

This resource requires authentication.

parameters.name: assetName
parameters.description: The unique identifying string that Marketplace uses to refer to this asset

LifetimeGmvMetricsRecord.description:
Optional GMV fields that may be present on each record when the request targets the `GMV` metric set.
`lifetimeGmvAppVersionType` is only returned when `LIFETIME_GMV` is part of the request, while `NON_HARMONISED_FORGE_VERSIONS_LIFETIME_GMV` and `STANDARD_FORGE_VERSIONS_LIFETIME_GMV` ignore the `attributesFilter` and are produced from predefined SQL.

name.description:
Attributes are inherent characteristics or properties associated with your data.
If no attribute is provided, we will generate aggregate data for all the apps based on the metrics specified in the request. 
It's important to note that a date specific attribute will always be present by default in the response. 

* `APP_KEY` - Unique key of the app. Available in the "Manage apps" portal. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `RECURRING_REVENUE`
  * `DISTRIBUTIONS`
  * `DOWNLOADS`
  * `FUNNEL_INSIGHTS`
  * `GMV`
* `APP_NAME` - Publicly displayed name of the app. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `RECURRING_REVENUE`
  * `FUNNEL_INSIGHTS`
* `GRACE_PERIOD_IN_DAYS` - The number of days before the license is considered as churned. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `RECURRING_REVENUE`
* `SUBSCRIPTION` - Type of subscription for the app which can be on monthly or annual basis. Possible Values are `TOTAL`, `MONTHLY` and `ANNUAL`. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `RECURRING_REVENUE`
* `APP_VERSION` - Version of the app. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `DISTRIBUTIONS`
  * `DOWNLOADS`
  * `GMV`
* `APP_VERSION_TYPE` - Classification for Forge versions. Possible values are `RUNS_ON_ATLASSIAN`, `NATIVE_APP`, or `NON_HARMONISED`. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `GMV`
* `PARENT_SOFTWARE` - Parent software name like - jira, confluence. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `DISTRIBUTIONS`
* `PARENT_SOFTWARE_VERSION` - Version of the parent software. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `DISTRIBUTIONS`
* `PARENT_SOFTWARE_MAJOR_VERSION` - Major version of the parent software. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `DISTRIBUTIONS`
* `PARENT_SOFTWARE_MINOR_VERSION` - Minor version of the parent software. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `DISTRIBUTIONS`
* `HOSTING` - Hosting of the app. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `DISTRIBUTIONS`
* `PARENT_PRODUCT` - Parent product name on which app is installed. Jira, Confluence. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `FUNNEL_INSIGHTS`
* `PRODUCT_TYPE` - Type of the product. Possible values are `paid`, `free`. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `FUNNEL_INSIGHTS`
* `PARENT_PRODUCT_TENURE_LEVEL` - Tenure level of the parent product. Possible values are `less_than_2_months`, `between_2_months_to_6_months`, `between_6_months_to_1_year`, `between_1_year_to_2_year`, `between_2_year_to_4_year`, `more_than_4_year`. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `FUNNEL_INSIGHTS`
* `PARENT_PRODUCT_EDITION` - Edition of the parent product. Possible values are `free`, `free_sandbox`, `free_standard`, `standard`, `standard_sandbox`, `premium`, `premium_sandbox`. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `FUNNEL_INSIGHTS`
* `PARENT_PRODUCT_UNIT_COUNT` - Tier of the parent product. Possible values are `1_to_10`, `11_to_100`, `101_to_1000`, `1001_to_10000`, `10001_to_100000`. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `FUNNEL_INSIGHTS`
* `LICENSE_STATUS` - Status of the license. Possible values are `active`, `inactive`. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `FUNNEL_INSIGHTS`
* `LICENSE_TYPE` - Type of the license. Possible values are `purchased`, `evaluation`, `churned`. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `FUNNEL_INSIGHTS`
* `PRODUCT_STATE` - State of the product. Possible values are `installed`, `not_installed`. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `FUNNEL_INSIGHTS`
* `PROSPECT_TYPE` - Type of the prospect. Possible values are `new`, `existing`, `lost`. Applicable MetricSets are:
  * `FUNNEL_INSIGHTS`

ReportingMetricTimeSeriesRequestMetrics.description: The predefined metrics requested and displayed. At least one of metricFields or metricSets must be provided

metricSets.description: The predefined metric sets (group of metric fields) requested and displayed.

name.description:
Indicates the metrics to be derived for a partner.

* `RECURRING_REVENUE` - The metric providing MRR/ARR data.
* `DISTRIBUTIONS` - The metric providing distributions data.
* `FUNNEL_INSIGHTS` - The metric providing funnel insights data.
* `DOWNLOADS` - The metric providing downloads data.
* `GMV` - The metric providing GMV (Gross Merchandise Value) data for apps.

metricFields.description: Predefined metrics fields requested and displayed.

name.description:
Fields to be filtered when fetching metric data.
* RECURRING_REVENUE:
  * `OPENING_MRR` - The closing MRR from the previous period (month) for the existing entitlements. 
  For example: The closing MRR on the 31st of March will be the opening MRR on 1st of April for the month of April.  
  * `NEW_MRR` - The MRR from new entitlements in any particular month.
  * `EXPANSION_MRR` -  The increase in MRR when customers pay more for existing entitlements. 
  This may be due to a change in the number of users, price increase or the removal of discounts. 
  For example: If customers who have 100 users switch to 150 users, the amount of MRR coming from this expansion is called expansion MRR.
  * `CONTRACTION_MRR` - The decrease in MRR when customers pay less for existing entitlements. 
  This may be due to a change in the number of users, price changes or the application of discounts.
  * `REACTIVATED_MRR` - Reactivation amount when a previously lapsed (or churned) entitlement is renewed.
  Reactivation is based on the previous maintenance end date. No limit on the time period is set to calculate reactivation. 
  * `EXPIRED_MRR` - The MRR of an expired (churned) entitlement, or one that moves from ‘paid’ to ‘free’ or that of a refunded amount.
  Churn is calculated purely based on the maintenance end date of a license. No grace period is being added while computing churn here.  
  * `CLOSING_MRR` - Opening MRR + New MRR + Expansion MRR + Reactivation MRR - Contraction MRR - Expired MRR.
    * But in case of App transfer/acquisition or change in billing frequency for the entitlement, above formula may not hold true. For example, if a customer changes their billing cycle from monthly to annual, then we won't be able to calculate the value of `CLOSING_MRR` using above formula.
  * `ARR` - Annual recurring revenue of the partner (calculated as Closing MRR * 12).
* DISTRIBUTIONS:
  * `INSTALL_COUNT` - Install counts for an app for specified time frame.
  * `USER_COUNT` - User counts for an app for specified time frame.
* FUNNEL_INSIGHTS:
  * `PROSPECT` - The number of prospects who have shown interest in the app.
  * `D1_D6_AI` - The average number of usage of app by an user during first 6 days of app install.
  * `AVERAGE_CHURN_DAY` - The average number of days it takes for a user to churn.
* DOWNLOADS:
  * `DOWNLOAD_COUNT` - Download counts for an app for specified time frame.
* GMV:
  * `LIFETIME_GMV` - Lifetime GMV filtered to a single `APP_VERSION_TYPE`. Provide exactly one `APP_VERSION_TYPE` filter (`RUNS_ON_ATLASSIAN`, `NATIVE_APP`, or `NON_HARMONISED`) when this field is requested; default is `NON_HARMONISED`; the API responds with `400` when the filter is invalid, or contains multiple values.
  * `NON_HARMONISED_FORGE_VERSIONS_LIFETIME_GMV` - Lifetime GMV for non-harmonised Forge versions. This breakout ignores the `attributesFilter` and is computed via predefined SQL.
  * `STANDARD_FORGE_VERSIONS_LIFETIME_GMV` - Lifetime GMV for Forge app versions that do not use connect modules (`has_connect_modules = false`). This breakout ignores the `attributesFilter` and is computed via predefined SQL.
  * GMV field names are case-insensitive. Requests that only specify `metricSets: [{ "name": "GMV" }]` receive all three fields for backward compatibility.

endDate.description:
* RECURRING_REVENUE: End date should be less than or equal to current date 
* DISTRIBUTIONS: End date should be less than current date
* FUNNEL_INSIGHTS: End date should be less than or equal to last day of previous month
* DOWNLOADS: End date should be less than current date
* GMV: End date should be less than current date

name.description:
Field name on which response needs to be sorted. Valid value for the metrics are listed below:
* `RECURRING_REVENUE`: `DATE`, `APP_KEY`, `APP_NAME`, `GRACE_PERIOD_IN_DAYS`, `SUBSCRIPTION`, `ARR`, `OPENING_MRR`, `NEW_MRR`, `EXPANSION_MRR`, `CONTRACTION_MRR`, `REACTIVATED_MRR`, `EXPIRED_MRR`, `CLOSING_MRR`
* `DISTRIBUTIONS`: `DATE`, `APP_KEY`, `APP_VERSION`, `HOSTING`, `PARENT_SOFTWARE`, `PARENT_SOFTWARE_VERSION`, `PARENT_SOFTWARE_MAJOR_VERSION`, `PARENT_SOFTWARE_MINOR_VERSION`, `INSTALL_COUNT`, `USER_COUNT`
* `FUNNEL_INSIGHTS`: `DATE`, `APP_KEY`, `APP_NAME`, `PARENT_PRODUCT`, `PRODUCT_TYPE`, `PARENT_PRODUCT_TENURE_LEVEL`, `PARENT_PRODUCT_EDITION`, `PARENT_PRODUCT_UNIT_COUNT`, `LICENSE_STATUS`, `LICENSE_TYPE`, `PRODUCT_STATE`, `PROSPECT_TYPE`, `PROSPECT`, `D1_D6_AI`, `AVERAGE_CHURN_DAY`
* `DOWNLOADS`: `DATE`, `APP_KEY`, `APP_VERSION`
* `GMV`: `DATE`, `LIFETIME_GMV`

date.description:
Start date of the month. 

 For example: For Dec 2021, date will be 2021-12-01

sale.description:
Sales value for that month (in USD), excluding Atlassian share of the sales. 

 For example: For Dec 2021, total sales was $2400 and Atlassian share is $400, sale will be 2000

previousMonthSale.description: Sales value for the previous month (in USD), excluding Atlassian share of the sales.

salesMoMGrowth.description:
Month over Month growth of sales, in percentage. 

 For example: Say sales was $1500 for Nov 2021 and $2000 for Dec 2021.

 Dec 2021 MoM growth = ((2000 - 1500) / 1500)*100 = 33.33%

salesPercentile.description: Shows, based on sales, partner’s percentile position as compared to all other partners on Marketplace.

salesMoMGrowthBenchmarkAllPartners.description:
Partner’s MoM sales growth compared against MoM growth of all partners in Marketplace.

 For example: Partner’s MoM sales growth for Dec 2021 is 33.33%

All Marketplace partners, say, had a total sales of $400K in Nov 2021 and $500K in Dec 2021.

All Marketplace partners' MoM growth = ((500K - 400K) / 400K)*100 = 25%.

Benchmark = 33.33% - 25% = +8.33%

salesYTD.description:
Year-to-date sales value (in USD), excluding Atlassian share of the sales.

For example: For Mar 2022, Year-to-date will mean Jan 2022 to Mar 2022.

Say total sales was $20K and Atlassian share is $3K, salesYTD will be 17000

salesYTDLastYear.description:
Year-to-date sales value for the corresponding period the previous year (in USD), excluding Atlassian share of the sales.

For example: For Mar 2022, Year-to-date previous year will mean Jan 2021 to Mar 2021.

Say total sales was $16K and Atlassian share is $2K, salesYTDLastYear will be 14000

salesYTDYoYGrowth.description:
Year over Year growth of sales, in percentage.

For example: Say for Mar 2022, YTD sales was $17K and YTD previous year sales was $14K.

Mar 2022 YTD YoY growth = ((17K - 14K) / 14K)*100 = +21.43%

salesYTDPercentile.description: Shows, based on YTD sales, partner’s percentile position as compared to all other partners on Marketplace.

salesYTDYoYGrowthBenchmarkAllPartners.description:
Partner’s YTD YoY sales growth compared against YTD YoY growth of all partners in Marketplace.

For example: Partner’s YTD YoY sales growth for Mar 2022 is 21.43%

All Marketplace partners, say, had a total sales of $800K between Jan 2022 to Mar 2022 and $750K between Jan 2021 to Mar 2021

All Marketplace partners' YTD YoY growth = ((800K - 750K) / 750K)*100 = 6.66%.

Benchmark = 21.43% - 6.66% = +14.77%

salesMoMGrowthBenchmarkAllAppsOnBaseProd.description:
App’s MoM sales growth compared to MoM growth of all apps on Marketplace (If the app is a Jira app, ‘all apps’ implies all Jira apps on Marketplace; If the app is a Confluence app, ‘all apps’ implies all Confluence apps on Marketplace).

For example: Say the app is a Jira app and had a MoM growth of 20% in Dec 2021.

All Jira apps on Marketplace generated sales of $240K in Nov 2021 and $300K in Dec 2021

All Jira apps' MoM growth = ((300K - 240K) / 240K)*100 = 25%

Benchmark = 20% - 25% = -5%

salesMoMGrowthBenchmarkBaseProduct.description:
App’s MoM sales growth compared to MoM growth of Base product (If the app is a Jira app, ‘Base product’ implies Jira; If the app is a Confluence app, ‘Base product’ implies Confluence).

For example: Say the app is a Jira app and had a MoM growth of 20% in Dec 2021.

Jira product generated sales of $15M in Nov 2021 and 13M in Dec 2021

Jira MoM growth = ((13M - 15M) / 15M)*100 = -13.33%

Benchmark = 20% - (-13.33%) = +33.33%

year.description: Year for which churn benchmark is being shared, in ‘YYYY’ format

month.description:
Month for which churn benchmark is being shared, in month number format.

 For example: '11' indicates November.

totalLicenses.description:
All app licenses which were active on the last date of month M are ‘totalLicenses’ for Month M.

 For example: If there are 100 active licenses as on 30th November, totalLicenses for November will be 100

churnedLicenses.description:
App licenses that were active based on the above definition in month M but not in Month M+1 are considered as churnedLicenses for the month M.

 For example: If there are 100 active licenses as on 30th November, of which 12 customers are not active on 31st December, churnedLicenses for November is 12.

churnRate.description:
This is Users churned (M+1)/total user count (M). % Active customers of Month M who didn’t renew their licenses in Month M+1 is considered as churn rate for Month M.

 For example: If there are 100 active licenses as on 30th November, of which 12 customers are not active on 31st December, churnRate for November is 12%

isolatedChurnRate.description:
When the app license churns but the parent product is still active.

 For example: If there are 100 active licenses as on 30th November, of which 12 customers are not active on 31st December.
Of the 12 customers, 8 customers’ parent product is still active and 4 customers’ parent product is not active on 31st December, isolatedChurnRate is (8/100) = 8%

churnRateBenchmark.description: Churn rate of the app as compared with all other apps in the product (Jira, Confluence)

isolatedChurnRateBenchmark.description: Isolated app churn of the app as compared with all other apps in the product (Jira, Confluence)

E.g. 

Say the app is a Jira app and had a YTD YoY evaluation growth of -8.33% in Mar 2022.

All Jira apps on Marketplace generated evaluations of 14K between Jan 2021 to Mar 2021 and 11K between Jan 2022 to Mar 2022

All Jira apps' MoM growth = ((11K - 14K) / 14K)*100 = -21.42%

Benchmark = -8.33% - (-21.42%) = +8.12%

date.description: It specifies the start date of the month of the concerned evaluation benchmark details, For example: "2021-01-01"

evalautionCount.description: Number of new evaluations for that particular vendor in that month. For example: "2021-12-01", if number of new evaluations for the vendor is 400, evaluationCount will be 400

evaluationMoMGrowth.description:
Month over Month growth of count of new evaluations, in percentage, For example: evaluation count was 380 for Nov 2021 and 400 for "2021-12-01".

 Dec 2021 MoM growth = ((400 - 380) / 380)*100 = 5.26%

evaluationPercentile.description: Shows, based on count of evaluations, for the particular vendor, its percentile position as compared to all apps on Marketplace.

evaluationMoMGrowthBenchmarkAllPartners.description:
Vendor’s MoM evaluation growth compared to MoM growth of all apps on Marketplace.

For example: 

 The app had a MoM evaluation growth of 5.26% in Dec 2021.

All apps on Marketplace generated evaluations of 7500 in Nov 2021 and 8000 in Dec 2021

All apps' MoM growth = ((8000 - 7500) / 7500)*100 = 6.66%

Benchmark = 5.26% - 6.66% = -1.4%

evaluationCountYTD.description:

For example: Mar 2022, Year-to-date will mean Jan 2022 to Mar 2022.

evaluationCountYTDLastYear.description:

 For example: Mar 2022, Year-to-date previous year will mean Jan 2021 to Mar 2021.

Say count of new evaluations during this period is 1200, evaluationCountYTDLastYear will be 1200

evaluationYTDYoYGrowth.description:
Year over Year growth of evaluation count, in percentage.

For example: Mar 2022, YTD evaluation count was 1100 and YTD previous year evaluation count was 1200.

Mar 2022 YTD YoY growth = ((1100 - 1200) / 1200)*100 = -8.33%

All apps' MoM growth = ((11K - 14K) / 14K)*100 = -21.42%

Benchmark = -8.33% - (-21.42%) = +8.12%

usersCount.description:
Number of customers who are using partner’s apps.

 For example: Say for Customer Insights By Region API  the usersCount would be the number of customers who are using partner’s apps in one of the regions (```apac, emea, americas, unknown```)

usersPercent.description:
Percentage of customers who are using partner’s apps.

 For example: Say for Customer Insights By Region API the usersPercent would be the percentage of customers who are using partner’s apps in one of the regions (```apac, emea, americas, unknown```) .

 Let's say 90 customers are using partner’s apps and 15 of them are from APAC region then usersPercent will be (15/90)*100  = 16.66

usersMarketplaceBenchmark.description:
usersPercent compared to usersPercent of overall Marketplace.

 For example: Say for Customer Insights By Region API the usersMarketplaceBenchmark would be usersPercent in one of the regions (```apac, emea, americas, unknown```) compared to usersPercent of overall Marketplace.

 Let's say Partner’s APAC users percent = 16.66%

Overall Marketplace has 3000 customers in total and 600 of them from APAC

APAC users percent of Marketplace = (600/3000)*100 = 20%

Index = 16.66 / 20 = 0.83

documents.title: About
documents.body:
The Marketplace API is a RESTful API that allows you to manage apps and your vendor account.

Unsure about the types exposed in this API? [Read more](/platform/marketplace/glossary/) about our terminology.

documents.title: Versioning
documents.body: Version 2 is the latest release of the Marketplace API. Scroll down to view the latest API documentation for this version.

documents.title: Authentication
documents.body: The Marketplace API uses [HTTP basic authentication](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Basic_access_authentication). The username is your Atlassian Account email and the password is a generated [API token](https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens). Once authenticated, you can view and modify most properties of your apps and your account. If you are not logged in, you are considered an unauthenticated user and will not be able to edit apps or vendor information.

documents.title: URI structure
documents.body:
All [Uniform Resource Identifiers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uniform_Resource_Identifier) (URIs) start with the following prefix: ``` bash https://marketplace.atlassian.com/rest/2 ``` When a resource provides a link to another resource, the link URI will not include the hostname (for instance, the link will be `/rest/2/addons` rather than `https://marketplace.atlassian.com/rest/2/addons`). URIs referring to content that is not provided by Atlassian Marketplace are always absolute (that is, they use *http://* or *https://*) and are encoded as ordinary string properties.
New Marketplace API URIs
Marketplace is coming up with new APIs and URIs start with the following prefix: ``` bash https://api.atlassian.com/rest/3 ``` These APIs can be accessed [here](/platform/marketplace/rest/v4/api-group-developer-space)

documents.title: Request format
documents.body:
All request and response representations are in JSON and use the content type `application/json` with the UTF-8 character set, except for `PATCH` requests.

The Marketplace API uses standard HTTP methods. Successful `POST`, `PUT`, and `PATCH` requests return a `Location` header in addition to the HTTP status codes documented below. Successful `DELETE` requests return only the status code. See the [*Errors* section](/platform/marketplace/rest/#errors) for information about responses that return an error.

|Method  |Description                                                                                        |Successful response|
|--------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|
|`GET`   |All `GET` requests are idempotent.                                                                 |200                |
|`POST`  |Creates a new entity.                                                                              |201                |
|`PUT`   |Modifies all editable properties of existing entity. It cannot be applied to a collection resource.|204                |
|`PATCH` |Modifies only selected properties of an existing entity. It cannot be applied to a collection resource. Request bodies use the [JSON Patch](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6902) document structure with the content type `application/json-patch+json`.|204|
|`DELETE`|Removes an entity. It cannot be applied to a collection resource and accepts no request body.      |204                |

documents.title: Pagination
Response format
documents.body:
All resources containing links to Atlassian Marketplace resources and web pages use the [HAL specification](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hypertext_Application_Language) with JSON (with some extensions as described below under `POST` and `PUT`, for cases that HAL does not cover). Such links will always be within a top-level `_links` or `_embedded` object. External links provided by third parties are considered simple data properties and can appear anywhere in a representation.

All resource links should:

- be relative to the host root, for example, `/rest/2/addons.`
- have a `self` link referring to themselves.

Links to the Marketplace website may be absolute or relative URIs. External links must always be absolute URIs.

When available, paginated responses provide the `next` and `prev` links to retrieve the next or previous page of items:

- `next` Retrieves the next page of items.
- `prev` Retrieves the previous page of items.

documents.title: Dates
documents.body: All dates referenced by the API are in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC).

documents.title: Errors
documents.body:
The following response status codes may be accompanied by an error representation.

| Status | Description                                                          |
|--------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 400    | Request body is malformed or has an illegal property value.          |
| 401    | Authentication is required.                                          |
| 403    | User was authenticated but is not authorized for this operation.     |
| 404    | Resource does not exist or is not visible to this user.              |
| 409    | Resource cannot be created or modified due to some state constraint. |
| 500    | Unexpected internal error in the Atlassian Marketplace.              |
| 502    | Unexpected error in an external service.                             |

Error responses may contain a JSON representation with a single top-level property, `errors`, that contains an array of objects (each with a message) and optional `path` and `code` properties. The `path` uses [JSON Pointer](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6901) format to refer to an invalid field in the request representation. If `path` is not present, the message describes a general condition that isn't tied to a specific field. If present, the `code` is a string that uniquely identifies the type of error. An example appears below:

``` javascript
{
    "errors": [
        { "message": "There is a general problem." },
        { "path": "/name", "message": "cannot be longer than 5000 characters" }
    ]
}
```
